teaser: Shenandoah Valley native, software engineer, musician, and lifelong maker who enjoys turning ideas into reality through code, creativity, and curiosity.
paragraphs: I'm a software and DevOps engineer by profession, a musician by instinct, and a maker because I can't seem to help myself.

I grew up in Virginia's Shenandoah Valley, where I learned to appreciate both the outdoors and the satisfaction of building things with my own hands. Since 2005 I've worked professionally in software engineering, but writing code has never just been a job for me. It's a creative medium—a paintbrush for turning ideas into reality.

Before software became my career, music was my world. I spent my twenties pursuing performance professionally after growing up playing jazz. Bass is still my primary instrument, whether it's electric or upright, though I've collected a few others along the way. I've also been fascinated with electronic music production since my teens, and more recently I've fallen down the wonderfully deep rabbit hole of modular synthesizers. Building modules from kits is just the beginning—I already have ideas for a few original designs I'd love to build.

Outside of technology and music, I like staying curious. I've visited every one of the lower 48 states, and I'm happiest when I'm hiking, backpacking, exploring somewhere new, or simply wandering through the woods with friends while pretending a round of disc golf is about the score. (It isn't.)
I also spend time boxing. I'm not especially good at it, but that's never been the point. Learning something difficult, improving a little at a time, and having fun along the way is reward enough.

I've always enjoyed helping friends and family tackle projects, whether that means solving a software problem, fixing something around the house, building something new, or just lending an extra set of hands. There's something deeply satisfying about making life a little easier for someone else.

When I'm not building things, you'll probably find me reading science fiction, watching a great sci-fi series, exploring a new game after a productive day, or enjoying a well-earned lazy weekend. I have a soft spot for just about every style of music, art, and storytelling because I believe there's usually something worthwhile to appreciate if you're willing to look for it.

One philosophy that's shaped me over the years is trying to see every issue from multiple perspectives. The world is complicated, people are interesting, and I think curiosity is more valuable than certainty.

Friends and family will always come first in my life. So does Boots, my cat, who earned permanent roommate status after I rescued her from oncoming traffic one day. She simply decided we lived together after that, and honestly, she made a pretty convincing argument.

Big Brown Moose Studios is where I collect the things I enjoy creating: software projects, electronics, music, experiments, and whatever else captures my imagination. I hope you'll find something here that inspires you to build something of your own.
